title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: This video will help you understand what a stroke is and the risk for having one.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Normally, blood vessels called arteries carry blood to the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: The brain needs a constant flow of blood to get the oxygen and nutrients it needs to work properly.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: During a stroke, also known as a brain attack, blood flow to part of the brain has stopped.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: The most common type of stroke is called ischemic stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: It can happen in an artery where a fatty substance called plaque has made the artery narrow.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: In one type of ischemic stroke called a thrombotic stroke, the plaque can break apart and form a blood clot

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: that blocks the flow of blood to the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: In another type of ischemic stroke called an embolic stroke, a blood clot forms in another area of the body and travels through the blood stream.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: If it reaches an artery near or in the brain, it can block the flow of blood.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: When an artery to part of the brain is blocked during a stroke, oxygen and nutrients in the blood can't get to those brain cells.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: The cells start to die within minutes.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: A less common type of stroke is called a hemorrhagic stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: It occurs when a weakened blood vessel breaks open and leaks blood into the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: This blocks blood flow to the brain and the leaking blood puts pressure on the brain tissue, which damages it.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: In a transient ischemic attack or TIA, a clot blocks the flow of blood to the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: But it's different from the other types of stroke because the clot breaks up after a short time.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Transient ischemic attacks usually don't cause any permanent damage.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: High blood pressure is the main risk factor for any type of stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Other risk factors include diabetes, certain types of heart disease, smoking, high cholesterol,

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: and having previous transient ischemic attacks.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: It's important to know these signs and symptoms of a stroke, a sudden numbness or weakness in your face, arms and legs, especially if it's on one side of your body.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Sudden confusion and trouble speaking or being unable to understand others speaking.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Sudden trouble seeing out of one or both eyes.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Sudden dizziness, loss of balance or being unable to walk.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: And a sudden severe headache.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: Call 911 right away if you think you or someone else may be having a stroke.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
text_en: To find out more about your risk for having a stroke, talk to your healthcare provider.
